intro: Real systems, shipped to production with a clear path for what ships next.

featured.title: Microservices Platform — 9 Services, Solo-Built
featured.subtitle: Cloud architecture · Rust · Python · React · Terraform · Google Cloud Run
featured.description: Designed and built a full microservices platform from scratch, then migrated deployment from Fly.io to Google Cloud: 7 Rust/Axum services, 1 Python/FastAPI AI orchestrator, and a React 19 frontend — now running on Cloud Run with Terraform-managed infrastructure, Cloud SQL Postgres, Secret Manager wiring, and GitHub Actions CI/CD.
highlights: 9 independently deployed services successfully running on Google Cloud Run
JWT-based authentication with cross-service token validation
Claude API integration for AI-powered planning via a dedicated orchestrator service
GitHub Actions CI pipeline with per-service Docker builds and automated deploys
Terraform-managed GCP baseline: Cloud Run + Cloud SQL + Artifact Registry + Secret Manager
Migration foundation in place for next iterations: observability, autoscaling tuning, and product feature expansion

others.title: DynamoDB Idempotency Prototype
others.subtitle: AWS CloudTrail · Rust · DynamoDB · Splunk HEC
others.description: A Rust prototype implementing exactly-once log delivery from AWS CloudTrail to a Splunk HEC endpoint. Uses DynamoDB conditional writes as a distributed idempotency lock, tracking per-event state (processing → done / failed) with TTL-based expiry and duplicate counting.
highlights: Atomic conditional PutItem ensures only one Lambda processes each event, even under retries
TTL-based record expiry keeps the table lean without manual cleanup
Duplicate counter tracks retry frequency for operational visibility
Status lifecycle: processing → done / failed, each with a timestamp
Full CRUD helpers abstracted for reuse across other DynamoDB-backed services

others.title: CloudTrail Medallion Data Pipeline
others.subtitle: Bronze/Silver/Gold · Rust Workers · DynamoDB Idempotency
others.description: Extension of the DynamoDB prototype into a medallion-style pipeline: Bronze captures raw CloudTrail events, Silver normalises and enriches records, Gold publishes analytics-ready security metrics. Idempotency keys and duplicate tracking remain the guardrail at ingestion.
highlights: Bronze layer preserves immutable raw events for auditability and replay
Silver layer applies schema normalisation, enrichment, and PII-safe shaping
Gold layer aggregates operational and security metrics for reporting
Ingestion keeps conditional-write idempotency so retries never double count
Structured to evolve into a standalone repo when implementation grows past prototype scope
